title: Your Card Makes You Spend 47% More
hook: Swiping a card literally shuts off the pain of spending in your brain — and it's costing you thousands.
angle: MIT neuroscience research shows credit/debit cards suppress the brain's 'pain of paying' signal, causing people to spend up to 47% more than cash users — a specific, surprising stat most people have never heard.
audience: 25–40 year-olds who use cards for everything and wonder where their money goes
key_numbers: Card users spend up to 47% more than cash users (MIT study)
Average American carries $6,200 in credit card debt
Switching discretionary spending to cash saves $3,000–$5,000 per year
